name: L-Sit Pull-Up
steps: Grip the bar with your hands shoulder-width apart and hang with your arms fully extended
Depress your shoulders, tighten your core, and keep your body still to avoid swinging
Raise your straight legs forward until they are parallel to the floor, forming an L-sit
Hold the L-sit position with your feet together and your legs as straight as your flexibility allows
Pull yourself up by driving your elbows down and back while keeping your legs in the L-sit
Continue pulling until your chin clears the bar without tucking your knees or losing the L shape
Lower yourself back down with control until your arms are fully extended, still holding the L-sit
Hang from the bar with your hands shoulder-width apart and your arms fully extended and your body relaxed
Keep your legs straight and your feet together
Depress your shoulders, tighten your core and avoid swinging your body
Slowly raise your legs forward while keeping them as straight as possible
Lift your legs until they are parallel to the floor or higher if your flexibility and strength allow
Slowly lower your legs back down to the starting position without swinging

name: Hanging L-Sit
steps: Grip the bar with your hands shoulder-width apart and hang with your arms fully extended
Depress your shoulders, tighten your core, and keep your body still to avoid swinging
Keep your legs straight and your feet together
Slowly raise your legs forward while keeping them as straight as possible
Lift your legs until they are parallel to the floor, forming an L-sit
Hold the position with your hips level and your legs active—avoid letting your feet drop or your back arch
When you finish the hold, lower your legs back down with control without swinging

name: Hanging Knee Raise
steps: Grip the bar with your hands shoulder-width apart and hang with your arms fully extended
Depress your shoulders, tighten your core, and keep your body still to avoid swinging
Slowly bend your knees and begin lifting them toward your chest
Keep your movement controlled—avoid swinging or using momentum
Raise your knees as high as you can while maintaining good form, ideally to hip level or higher
Slowly lower your legs back down to the starting hanging position

name: Hanging High Knee Raise
steps: Grip the bar with your hands shoulder-width apart and hang with your arms fully extended
Depress your shoulders, tighten your core, and keep your body still to avoid swinging
Bend your knees and drive them up toward your chest as high as you can using your core
Aim to raise your knees above your hips while keeping the movement slow and controlled
Pause briefly at the top without using momentum
Slowly lower your legs back down to the starting hanging position

name: Upside Down Deadlift
steps: Grip the bar with your hands shoulder-width apart and hang with your arms fully extended
Depress your shoulders and tighten your core
Lift your straight legs until your body forms an inverted pike with your hips above your head
Keep your arms straight and your shoulders active throughout the movement
Slowly lower your hips and torso as if hinging at the hips while keeping your legs straight
Lower until your torso is roughly parallel to the floor or as far as you can control without arching your lower back
Pull your hips back up to the inverted pike position using your core and lats
Lower your legs back down with control to finish the rep
Grip the bar with your hands shoulder-width apart using an overhand grip
Hang from the bar with your arms fully extended and your shoulders engaged—pull them slightly down from your ears
Tighten your core and keep your legs still to avoid swinging
Pull yourself up by driving your elbows down and back toward your ribs
Lead with your chest and keep your shoulders down as you rise
Continue pulling until your chin clears the bar
Lower yourself slowly back to a full hang with control
Grip the bar with your hands shoulder-width apart using an underhand grip with your palms facing you
Hang with your arms fully extended and engage your shoulders by pulling them slightly down from your ears
Tighten your core and keep your legs still to avoid swinging
Pull yourself up by driving your elbows down and back toward your ribs
Lead with your chest and keep your shoulders down as you rise
Continue pulling until your chin clears the bar
Lower yourself slowly back to a full hang with control
Grip the bar with your hands shoulder-width apart and hang with your arms fully extended
Engage your core and depress your shoulders
Tuck your knees toward your chest and begin leaning your upper body back
Keep pulling your shoulder blades down and back as your torso becomes more horizontal
Open your hips slightly while keeping your knees tucked and your back rounded forward
Hold your body roughly parallel to the floor with your arms straight and your face toward the ceiling
Keep your core tight and avoid letting your hips sag or pike
When you finish the hold, lower your legs back down with control
Grip the bar with your hands shoulder-width apart and hang with your arms fully extended
Engage your core, glutes, and lats and depress your shoulders
Raise your legs and lean back until your body is horizontal with your face toward the ceiling
Keep your arms straight and locked, with your shoulder blades protracted and your elbows turned slightly outward
Hold your legs together and straight in line with your torso
Maintain a slight posterior pelvic tilt so your lower back stays flat and your hips do not sag
Breathe steadily while keeping tension through your entire body
When you finish the hold, tuck your knees and lower yourself with control
Grip the bar with your hands shoulder-width apart and hang with your arms fully extended
Engage your core and pull your shoulder blades down and back
Raise into your strongest front lever position—tucked, advanced tuck, or full—using controlled momentum only if needed
Begin lowering your body by opening at the shoulders while keeping your arms straight
Lower slowly as far as you can while maintaining tension through your core and lats
Stop before your form breaks down, then pull yourself back up into the lever position
Complete the rep with control and reset your body tension before the next rep

name: Planche
steps: Place your hands on the floor shoulder-width apart with your fingers spread for stability pointing outward
Start in a push-up position and protract your shoulders by pushing the floor away
Lean your shoulders forward past your wrists while keeping your arms straight
Tuck your pelvis and tighten your core, glutes, and legs
Lift your feet off the floor and hold your body parallel to the ground
Keep your head neutral and look slightly ahead of your hands
Maintain forward lean and full-body tension throughout the hold
When you finish the hold, lower your feet back to the floor with control

name: L-Sit Kicks
description: Start in a tucked L-sit position and move your legs forwards into the L-Sit position
steps: Sit on the floor with your legs extended and your hands placed beside your hips
Press down through your hands to lift your hips off the floor
Start in a tucked L-sit by bending your knees toward your chest while keeping your feet off the ground
Keep your chest upright and your shoulders depressed away from your ears
Slowly extend your knees and kick your legs forward toward a full L-sit
Straighten your legs as much as your flexibility and strength allow while keeping your hips lifted
Return your knees to the tucked position with control to complete one rep

name: Tucked L-Sit
steps: Sit on the floor with your hands placed beside your hips, fingers pointing forward
Press down through your hands and straighten your arms to lift your hips off the floor
Bend your knees and tuck them toward your chest while keeping your feet off the ground
Lean your torso slightly back and keep your chest lifted
Depress your shoulders and engage your core to hold the position
Keep your hips level and avoid letting your feet drop toward the floor
Hold the tucked L-sit with steady breathing and full-body tension
When you finish the hold, lower your hips back to the floor with control

name: L-Sit
steps: Sit on the floor with your hands placed beside your hips, fingers pointing forward
Press down through your hands and straighten your arms to lift your hips off the floor
Extend your legs forward with your knees straight and your feet together
Point your toes and raise your legs until they are parallel to the floor or as high as you can hold with good form
Lean your torso slightly back and keep your chest lifted
Depress your shoulders and squeeze your core, quads, and glutes to maintain the hold
Avoid rounding your lower back or letting your hips drop
When you finish the hold, lower your hips and legs back to the floor with control

description: Perform the push up from a kneeling position rather than on your toes. This will reduce the amount of body weight you need to lift
steps: Start on your hands and knees with your hands shoulder-width apart and directly under your shoulders
Walk your knees back slightly so your body forms a straight line from your head to your knees
Tighten your core and glutes to keep your hips from sagging
Slowly bend your elbows and lower your chest toward the floor
Keep your elbows at roughly a 45-degree angle to your body rather than flaring straight out
Lower yourself until your chest is just above the floor or as far as you can with good form
Push through your palms to straighten your arms and return to the starting position
Keep your neck neutral by looking slightly ahead of your hands
Start in a high push-up position with your hands shoulder-width apart directly under your shoulders
Extend your legs behind you and balance on your toes with your body in a straight line from head to heels
Tighten your core and glutes to keep your hips from sagging or piking up
Slowly bend your elbows and lower your chest toward the floor
Keep your elbows at roughly a 45-degree angle to your body rather than flaring straight out
Lower yourself until your chest is just above the floor or as far as you can with good form
Push through your palms to straighten your arms and return to the starting position
Keep your neck neutral by looking slightly ahead of your hands

name: Decline Push Up
steps: Place your hands on the floor shoulder-width apart and set your feet on a sturdy raised surface behind you
Walk your hands back until your upper body is angled down and your shoulders are stacked over your wrists
Keep your body in a straight line from your head through your hips to your feet
Tighten your core and glutes before you begin the movement
Slowly bend your elbows and lower your upper chest toward the floor
Keep your elbows at roughly a 45-degree angle to your body and avoid flaring them straight out
Lower yourself until your chest is just above the floor or as far as you can with good form
Push through your palms to straighten your arms and return to the starting position

name: Reverse Leg Raises
description: Lay your stomach on a raised surface and raise your legs into a horizontal position
steps: Lie face down on a bench or raised surface with your hips at the edge and your legs hanging off
Grip the sides of the bench for stability and keep your upper body relaxed on the surface
Engage your glutes and lower back and keep your legs straight with your feet together
Slowly raise your legs until they are parallel to the floor or as high as you can control
Hold briefly at the top without arching your lower back excessively
Lower your legs back down with control to complete one rep

name: Planche Lean
description: Make sure your hands up at your waist position
steps: Start in a high push-up position with your hands shoulder-width apart and arms fully straight
Engage your core and glutes to keep your body in a straight line from head to heels
Push through the floor and protract your shoulders by rounding the upper back slightly
Slowly shift your shoulders forward past your wrists while keeping your elbows locked
Lean as far forward as you can while maintaining control and proper form
Keep your head neutral and look slightly ahead of your hands

name: Tuck Planche
steps: Place your hands on the floor shoulder-width apart with your fingers spread for stability pointing outwaards
Start in a push-up position and protract your shoulders by pushing the floor away
Lean your shoulders forward past your wrists while keeping your arms straight
Tuck your knees toward your chest and protract your shoulders slightly
Lift your feet off the floor and balance on your hands with your hips level
Keep your core tight and your knees tucked close to your body
Maintain forward lean and steady breathing throughout the hold
When you finish the hold, lower your feet back to the floor with control

name: Wall Sit
steps: Start by getting onto the floor on your hands and knees
Step your feet forward roughly 1–2 feet away from the wall
Slowly slide your back down the wall by bending your knees
Lower yourself until your thighs are parallel to the floor and your knees form roughly 90-degree angles
Keep your knees directly above your ankles and avoid letting them move inward
Press your back and shoulders firmly against the wall
Tighten your core and keep your chest upright
description: Sit in an imaginary chair pressing your backing into the wall. Your things should be parallel to the ground.

name: Plank
steps: Start by getting onto the floor on your hands and knees
Place your forearms on the ground with your elbows directly under your shoulders
Extend your legs straight behind you and balance on your toes
Keep your body in a straight line from your head to your heels
Tighten your core, glutes, and legs to prevent your hips from sagging or lifting too high
Keep your neck neutral by looking down at the floor slightly ahead of your hands
Start in a push-up position with your hands placed close together beneath your chest
Position your thumbs and index fingers so they touch, forming a diamond shape.
Extend your legs behind you and balance on your toes.
Keep your body in a straight line from your head to your heels
Tighten your core and glutes to maintain stability
Slowly bend your elbows and lower your chest toward your hands
Keep your elbows close to your body as you descend
Lower yourself until your chest is just above your hands or as far as you can comfortably go
Push through your palms to straighten your arms and return to the starting position
Keep your neck neutral by looking slightly ahead of your hands

name: Wide Push Up
steps: Start in a push-up position with your hands placed wider than shoulder-width apart
Slowly bend your elbows and lower your chest toward the floor
Keep your elbows flaring out slightly more than a standard push-up, but stay controlled
Lower yourself until your chest is just above the ground or as far as you can comfortably go.
Push through your palms to straighten your arms and return to the starting position

name: Archer Push Up
steps: Start in a standard push-up position with your hands placed wider than shoulder-width apart with hands pointing outward
Keep your legs straight and balance on your toes with your body in a straight line.
Tighten your core and glutes to stabilize your body
Shift your weight to one side while bending that arm, keeping the opposite arm straight
Lower your chest toward the bent arm, as if doing a one-sided push-up
Keep the straight arm fully extended and use it for light support only
Push through the bent arm to return to the center position
Repeat the movement on the opposite side, alternating each rep
Keep your hips level and avoid twisting your torso too much

name: Bent Arm Planche
steps: Place your hands on the floor shoulder-width apart and lean your shoulders well forward past your wrists
Bend your elbows to about 90 degrees while keeping your forearms vertical
Tuck your pelvis, engage your core and glutes, and lift your feet off the floor
Hold your body roughly parallel to the ground with your legs together
Keep pressing the floor away to maintain shoulder protraction and stability
Avoid letting your hips sag or your elbows drift backward
Breathe steadily while maintaining full-body tension
When you finish the hold, lower your feet back to the floor with control

name: Elbow Lever
steps: Start in a push up position with your legs straddled with your hands beside your hips with your fingers pointing outward
Place your elbows against your lower abdomen or hips and lean your torso forward
Press down through your hands and straighten your arms to lift your hips and legs off the floor
Keep your legs together and extend them forward until your body is roughly horizontal
Balance on your hands with your face toward the floor and your core fully engaged
Keep your shoulders protracted and avoid collapsing through your lower back
Hold the position with steady breathing and active tension through your entire body
When you finish the hold, lower your hips and legs back to the floor with control

name: One Arm Elbow Lever
steps: Start in a push up position with your legs straddled with your hands beside your hips with your fingers pointing outward
Place your working elbow against your lower abdomen or hip and lean your torso forward
Extend your free arm out to the side or behind your back for balance
Press down through your working hand and straighten your arm to lift your hips and legs off the floor
Keep your legs together and extend them forward until your body is roughly horizontal
Hold the position with your core tight and your shoulders stable
Avoid twisting your hips or collapsing through your lower back
When you finish the hold, lower yourself back to the floor with control

name: Bench Dip
steps: Sit on the edge of a sturdy bench or chair and place your hands beside your hips, gripping the edge with your hands
Position your hands shoulder-width apart with your fingers pointing forward
Slide your hips off the front of the bench while keeping your arms straight
Extend your legs out in front of you with your heels resting on the floor
Keep your chest lifted and shoulders depressed away from your ears
Slowly bend your elbows and lower your body toward the floor
Lower yourself until your elbows reach about a 90-degree angle
Keep your elbows pointing backward rather than flaring outward
Push through your palms to straighten your arms and raise yourself back to the starting position
Grip the parallel bars with your palms facing inward
Jump or step up to a support hold with your arms straight and your shoulders depressed away from your ears
Lean your torso slightly forward and cross your ankles behind you if needed for stability
Slowly bend your elbows and lower your body between the bars
Lower until your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor or your shoulders reach a comfortable depth
Keep your elbows tracking back rather than flaring straight out to the sides
Press down through your palms to straighten your arms and return to the top position
Finish each rep with your shoulders down and your body controlled, not swinging
Grip the parallel bars and press up to a straight-arm support hold with your shoulders depressed
Keep your torso upright with minimal forward lean
Cross your ankles behind you if needed for stability
Slowly bend your elbows straight back along your sides
Lower until your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor or your shoulders reach a comfortable depth
Keep your elbows close to your body throughout the movement
Press down through your palms to straighten your arms and return to the top position
Finish each rep with your shoulders down and your body controlled, not swinging

name: Wall Walk ins
description: Start in a push up position with your feet against the wall and walk your feet up the wall and your hands towards it
steps: Start in a push-up position facing away from a wall with your feet on the ground
Place your feet on the wall at hip height and walk your hands slightly closer to the wall
Keep your arms straight and your core tight as you begin walking your feet up the wall
Walk your hands in toward the wall at the same pace so your body stays stacked and controlled
Continue until your torso is nearly vertical with your shoulders over your hands
Hold briefly at the top if you can maintain tension through your core and shoulders
Walk your feet back down the wall and your hands away from the wall with control to finish the rep

description: Perform a handstand with your feet against the wall
steps: Stand facing the wall and lean forward to assume a pike position
Place your hands shoulder-width apart with your fingers spread and your arms fully straight
Kick your legs up towards the wall so that so that they land against the wall
Stack your shoulders over your wrists and push the floor away to keep your shoulders active
Squeeze your core, glutes, and legs together to maintain a straight body line
Point your toes and keep only light contact with the wall for balance
Look at the space between your hands and avoid arching your lower back
Hold the position with steady breathing and full-body tension
When you finish the hold, walk your feet down the wall with control
Start in a standing straddle and place your hands on the floor shoulder-width apart about a foot in front of your feet
Kick up with control or tuck and press into a handstand, bringing your legs together overhead
Stack your shoulders over your wrists and keep your arms fully straight
Squeeze your core, glutes, and legs together to maintain a straight, hollow body line
Push the floor away and keep your fingers active for balance
Look at the space between your hands and avoid arching your lower back
Make small adjustments with your fingers and shoulders to stay balanced
When you finish the hold, come down with control into a tuck or straddle landing
Start in a standard push-up position with your hands shoulder-width apart
Walk your hands backwards until your upper body is straight
Lean forwards so your weight is resting on your hands an not your feet
Keep your elbows angled slightly backward rather than flaring straight out
Start in a pike position with your hands shoulder-width apart and your hips high
Keep your arms straight and your legs as straight as your flexibility allows
Press the floor away and set your shoulders in a strong, active position
Without bending your elbows, let your shoulder blades rise slightly toward your ears
Push the floor away and depress your shoulders back down as far as you can control
Keep your core tight so your hips do not drop during each rep
Complete the rep with your arms still straight and your body stable

name: Pike Push Up
steps: Start in a pike position with your hands shoulder-width apart and your hips high above your shoulders
Keep your legs straight and your weight shifted forward onto your hands
Tighten your core and look at the space between your feet or slightly back toward your feet
Bend your elbows and lower the top of your head toward the floor between your hands
Keep your elbows angled slightly backward rather than flaring straight out
Lower as far as you can while maintaining a pike shape through your hips
Press through your palms to straighten your arms and return to the starting pike position

name: Pseudo Planche Push Up
steps: Start in a push-up position and walk your feet forwards until your hands are beside your hips or waist
Turn your fingers outward and lean your shoulders forward past your wrists
Protract your shoulders by pushing the floor away and rounding your upper back slightly
Keep your core tight and your legs straight behind you
Bend your elbows and lower your chest forward and down while maintaining the forward lean
Keep your elbows close to your body and avoid letting your hips sag
Press through your palms to straighten your arms and return to the top position
Finish each rep with your shoulders still leaning forward and your body in one straight line

name: Boat Hold
steps: Sit on the floor with your knees bent and your feet flat
Lean back slightly and lift your feet off the floor while keeping your chest lifted
Straighten your legs and raise them until they form a V shape with your torso
Reach your arms forward parallel to the floor or alongside your legs
Balance on your sit bones and keep your lower back long, not rounded
Engage your core and quads to hold your legs at roughly eye level or higher
Breathe steadily while keeping your shoulders relaxed and away from your ears
When you finish the hold, lower your feet back to the floor with control

name: Hollow Hold
steps: Lie on your back with your arms extended overhead and your legs straight and together
Press your lower back firmly into the floor by tucking your pelvis slightly
Lift your shoulder blades slightly off the floor while keeping your arms by your ears
Raise your straight legs a few inches off the floor without letting your lower back arch
Hold your body in a banana shape with your ribs and hips drawing toward each other
Keep your core tight and your legs active with pointed toes
Breathe steadily while maintaining tension through your entire body
When you finish the hold, lower your shoulders and legs back to the floor with control

name: Seated Leg Raise
steps: Sit on the floor with your legs extended and your hands placed beside your hips for support
Lean back slightly and engage your core while keeping your chest lifted
Keep your knees straight and your feet together
Slowly raise your straight legs as high as you can control
Avoid rocking backward or using momentum from your arms
Hold briefly at the top without letting your lower back round excessively
Lower your legs back down with control to complete one rep

name: Straddle Leg Raises
steps: Sit on the floor in a wide straddle with your legs as straight as your flexibility allows
Place your hands on the floor in front of you for support
Lean back slightly and engage your core while keeping your chest lifted
Slowly raise both straight legs off the floor while keeping them in a straddle position
Lift until your legs are roughly parallel to the floor or as high as you can hold with good form
Keep your knees pointing upward and avoid collapsing inward at the hips
Hold the position with steady breathing and active tension through your legs and core
When you finish the hold, lower your legs back to the floor with control
Grip the parallel bars and press up to a straight-arm support position with your shoulders depressed
Keep your arms straight and your body still before you begin the movement
Tighten your core and avoid swinging your torso
Slowly raise your straight legs forward in front of you while keeping your feet together
Lift your legs until they are parallel to the floor or as high as your strength and flexibility allow
Pause briefly at the top without losing tension in your shoulders or core
Lower your legs back down with control to the starting support position
Grip the bar with your hands shoulder-width apart and hang with your arms fully extended
Engage your shoulders by pulling them slightly down and away from your ears
Tighten your core and avoid swinging before you begin the movement
Raise your legs by flexing at the hips, keeping them as straight as your flexibility allows
Continue lifting until your toes touch or pass the bar
Use controlled momentum only if needed, but prioritize a smooth leg raise over kipping
Lower your legs back down with control to a full hang to complete one rep

name: Dead Hang
steps: Grip the bar with your hands shoulder-width apart using an overhand grip
Hang with your arms fully extended and let your body relax into the stretch
Allow your shoulders to rise slightly toward your ears in a passive hang
Keep your legs together and avoid swinging
Breathe steadily and hold the position without gripping excessively hard
When you finish the hold, step down or drop off with control rather than jumping down abruptly
Grip the bar with your hands shoulder-width apart and hang with your arms fully extended
Engage your core and raise your legs into a tucked L-sit with your knees bent toward your chest
Pull your body up and back while keeping your knees tucked
Move toward a tuck front lever with your torso becoming more horizontal
Lower back down through the tuck with control, reversing the path you took on the way up
Return to a tucked hang or tucked L-sit without swinging between reps
Keep your arms straight throughout and drive the movement from your core and lats
Grip the bar with your hands shoulder-width apart and hang with your arms fully extended
Engage your core and depress your shoulders
Pull your body up until your chin is above the bar keeping your body straight
Straighten your arms while moving your shoulders back so that your body moves to a horizontal position
Pull your body up and back while tucking your knees to move toward a tuck front lever
Extend your hips and open into a horizontal position with your face toward the ceiling
Return to a full hang without swinging between reps

name: Dragon Flag
steps: Lie on the ground and grip something behind you to give leverage
Press your upper back into the ground and lift your hips and legs off the surface
Raise your body until you form a straight line from your shoulders through your hips to your feet
Keep your core tight and your lower back flat so your body moves as one rigid unit
Slowly lower your body toward the ground while maintaining the straight line
Stop before your hips touch down if your form begins to break, then pull back up to the top position
Complete the rep with control and reset your grip and tension before the next rep
Grip the bar with your hands shoulder-width apart and hang with your arms fully extended
Engage your core and pull your shoulder blades down and back
Tuck your knees and rotate backward through an inverted hang, keeping your arms straight
Continue rotating until your body is horizontal with your face toward the ceiling
Keep your hips open and your body in one straight line from shoulders to feet
Maintain tension through your core and lats throughout the hold
Avoid piking or arching excessively at the lower back
When you finish the hold, reverse the rotation with control back to a hang
Hang from the bar with your arms fully extended and your core engaged
Tuck your knees and lift your hips to begin rotating backward through an inverted position
Continue the rotation with straight arms until you pass through a full invert
Lower your feet toward the ground behind you as far as you can control with tension
Reverse the movement by pulling your legs back up and rotating forward to return to a hang
Keep the entire motion slow and controlled, using your core and lats rather than momentum
Finish each rep in a stable hang before starting the next rotation

name: Planche Push Up
steps: Place your hands on the floor shoulder-width apart and lean your shoulders well forward past your wrists
Lift your feet off the floor into a planche or advanced tuck planche position with your arms straight
Keep your core, glutes, and legs tight so your body stays parallel to the ground
Bend your elbows and lower your body forward and down while maintaining the forward lean
Keep your elbows close to your body and avoid letting your hips drop
Press through your palms to straighten your arms and return to the top planche position
Finish each rep with your shoulders still leaning forward and your body in one straight line

name: Handstand Press
steps: Start in a deep pike or straddle stand with your hands on the floor shoulder-width apart in front of your feet
Lean forward and shift your weight onto your hands while keeping your arms straight
Press through your shoulders and lift your hips high as your feet leave the ground
Continue pressing until your body stacks vertically in a handstand
Control the descent by reversing the motion, lowering your feet back toward the floor with a pike shape
Keep your core tight throughout so you do not collapse through your lower back
Complete the rep when both feet return to the floor with control

name: Handstand Push Up
steps: Kick up or press into a handstand with your arms straight and your body in a straight line
Stack your shoulders over your wrists and push the floor away to keep your shoulders active
Tighten your core and glutes to maintain a hollow body position
Bend your elbows and lower your head toward the floor between your hands
Keep your elbows angled slightly forward rather than flaring straight out to the sides
Lower as far as you can while staying balanced and keeping your hips stacked over your hands
Press through your palms to straighten your arms and return to a full handstand
Grip the bar with one hand using an overhand grip
Hang with your arms fully extended and engage your core to minimize swinging
Pull with your working arm by driving your elbow down and back while the assist arm stays nearly straight
Shift your weight toward your working side and keep your shoulders level
Continue pulling until your working-side shoulder rises near the bar or your chin passes beside your working hand
Lower yourself slowly back to a full hang with control
Alternate sides each rep or complete all reps on one side before switching

name: Human Flag
steps: Grip a vertical pole or bar with your top hand in an overhand grip and your bottom hand in an underhand grip
Place your top hand at roughly head height and your bottom hand lower on the pole, about hip height
Engage your core and press your bottom arm while pulling with your top arm to lift your body sideways
Keep your arms straight and stack your shoulders so your body forms a horizontal line parallel to the ground
Squeeze your glutes and legs together to maintain a rigid body line
Keep your hips level and avoid letting your lower back arch or your legs drop
Hold the position with steady breathing and full-body tension
When you finish the hold, lower yourself back down with control

name: L-Sit to Handstand
steps: Grip the parallel bars and press up to a support hold with your arms straight
Lift into an L-sit with your legs straight and parallel to the floor
Lean your shoulders forward and compress your body by bringing your hips over your hands
Press through your palms and lift your hips upward while keeping your arms straight
Continue pressing until your body stacks vertically in a handstand on the bars
Control the descent by reversing the motion back through the L-sit
Keep your core tight throughout so you do not swing or collapse through your lower back
Complete the rep when you return to a stable L-sit or support hold

name: Bent Arm Tuck Planche
steps: Place your hands on the parallettes shoulder-width apart and start in a support hold with straight arms
Lean your shoulders forward past your wrists while keeping your core tight
Bend your elbows to about 90 degrees and tuck your knees toward your chest
Lift your feet off the floor and balance with your hips level and your back slightly rounded
Keep pressing the bars away to maintain shoulder protraction and stability
Hold the bent-arm tuck position without letting your hips drop or your elbows drift backward
Breathe steadily while maintaining full-body tension
When you finish the hold, lower your feet back to the floor with control
Grip the rings and lift yourself to a support hold with your arms straight at your sides
Turn the rings out and depress your shoulders away from your ears
Slowly lower your body by allowing your arms to open out to the sides until they are roughly parallel to the floor
Keep your elbows straight and your core tight throughout the descent
Hold the iron cross position with your shoulders down and your body stable
Avoid shrugging your shoulders up or bending your elbows
Press back up through your arms to return to the support hold with control
When you finish the hold, lower yourself down from the rings safely
Grip the bar with your hands shoulder-width apart and hang with your arms fully extended
Keep your arms straight throughout the entire movement
Without bending your elbows, pull your shoulder blades down and back by engaging your upper back
Hold the depressed position briefly at the bottom of the movement
Allow your shoulders to rise slightly toward your ears in a controlled shrug
Repeat the up-and-down motion using only your shoulder blades
Keep your core engaged and avoid swinging your body
Start in a side plank on one hand with your arm straight and your hand directly under your shoulder
Stack your feet or place your top foot in front of your bottom foot for stability
Lift your hips high so your body forms a straight line from your head to your feet
Reach your top arm toward the ceiling or place it on your hip
Press the floor away through your bottom hand and keep your shoulder away from your ear
Tighten your core and glutes to prevent your hips from sagging
Hold the position with steady breathing
When you finish the hold, lower your hips with control and switch sides
Grip the bar with your hands wider than shoulder-width apart using an overhand grip
Hang with your arms fully extended and engage your core to minimize swinging
Pull yourself up while shifting your weight toward one side and bending that arm
Keep the opposite arm straighter, using it for light support only
Pull until your working-side shoulder rises near the bar or your chin passes beside your working hand
Lower yourself with control back to center before switching sides
Keep your shoulders down and avoid twisting your torso excessively
Alternate sides each rep or complete all reps on one side before switching
Grip the bar with your hands placed wider than shoulder-width apart using an overhand grip
Hang with your arms fully extended and engage your shoulders by pulling them slightly down from your ears
Tighten your core and keep your legs still to avoid swinging
Pull yourself up by driving your elbows down and back
Continue pulling until your chin clears the bar
Lower yourself slowly back to a full hang with control

Engage your core and raise your straight legs until they are parallel to the floor
Keep your legs together and your lower back stable
Rotate your legs to one side while keeping your shoulders square and your arms straight
Move through the center and continue to the opposite side in a controlled arc
Keep the movement slow and avoid using momentum from your hips or shoulders
Return to the center position to complete one rep
Start in a high side plank on one hand with your arm straight and your hips lifted
Stack your feet or place your top foot in front of your bottom foot for stability
Keep your bottom shoulder pressed down and away from your ear
Slowly lower your hips toward the floor without bending your supporting arm
Stop before your form breaks down, then drive your hips back up to the high side plank position
Keep your core tight and your body in a straight line throughout the movement
Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other
Start in a high side plank on one hand with your other arm holding onto a vertical pole
Shift your weight onto your bottom arm and begin lifting both feet off the floor
Keep your legs together and extend your body into a straight horizontal line
Squeeze your core and glutes to hold your hips level with your shoulders
Keep tension and pull on the pole with your top hand without lifting your legs
Start on your hands and knees with your hands shoulder-width apart directly under your shoulders

Walk your knees back so your body forms a straight line from your head to your knees
Tighten your core, glutes, and legs to keep your hips from sagging or lifting too high
Hold your body in a rigid plank position through your knees and torso
Keep your neck neutral by looking down at the floor slightly ahead of your hands
Breathe steadily while maintaining tension
When you finish the hold, lower yourself to the floor with control
Grip the bar with your hands shoulder-width apart and hang with your arms fully extended
Engage your core and pull your knees toward your chest to begin the movement
Pull your hips up toward the bar while leaning your torso back slightly
Continue pulling until your hips rise above the bar and your torso spins aroundthe bar
Shift your weight forward and press down to arrive in a support hold on top of the bar
Lower yourself back over the bar with control by leaning forward and reversing the path
Return to a hang with straight arms to complete one rep
Avoid using excessive kipping; prioritize a smooth pull with your lats and core
Stand on one leg with your other leg extended straight in front of you
Extend your arms forward for balance and keep your chest lifted
Sit your hips back and down on your standing leg while keeping your extended leg off the floor

Keep your extended leg straight and your standing foot flat on the floor
Drive through your standing heel to stand back up to the starting position
Complete all reps on one leg before switching sides

Keep your chest lifted and engage your core

Lower until your thighs are parallel to the floor or as deep as you can with good form
Keep your knees tracking in line with your toes and avoid collapsing inward

Keep your chest lifted and engage your core for balance

Sit your hips back and down on your bent leg while keeping your straight leg extended

Drive through the heel of your bent leg to stand back up to center
Alternate sides each rep or complete all reps on one side before switching

Shift all of your weight to your front leg and stand up without using your back leg

Keep your elbows at roughly a 45-degree angle to your body
Press through your palms to straighten your arms and return to the starting position
Stand in front of a low bar at about waist height and grip it with an overhand grip slightly less than shoulder width

Keep your upper arms stationary and lower until your forehead nears the bar

Set a low bar at about waist height and lie underneath it

Hang with your arms fully extended and engage your core to minimize swinging

Quickly transition by rotating your wrists and shifting your weight over the bar
Press down to straighten your arms and finish in a support hold on top of the bar
Lower yourself back to a hang with control or step down safely between reps

description: A pull up where you continue past getting your chin above the bar
steps: Grip the bar with your hands shoulder-width apart using an overhand grip
Hang with your arms fully extended and engage your core to minimize swinging
Pull explosively by driving your elbows down and back
Continue pulling past chin height toward your chest, stomach, or hips depending on your progression
Use controlled hip drive only as much as needed to reach the target height
Lower yourself back to a full hang with control to complete one rep
Position yourself in the captain's chair with your back against the pad and your forearms on the rests
Grip the handles and depress your shoulders away from your ears
Keep your legs straight and your feet together
Tighten your core and avoid swinging your torso
Slowly raise your straight legs until they are parallel to the floor or higher
Pause briefly at the top without using momentum
Lower your legs back down with control to complete one rep
Lie on your back with your knees bent and your feet flat on the floor hip-width apart
Place your hands lightly behind your head or across your chest
Tighten your core and press your lower back gently into the floor
Lift your shoulder blades off the floor by curling your upper body toward your knees
Keep your neck neutral and avoid pulling on your head with your hands
Pause briefly at the top while maintaining tension in your abs
Lower your shoulders back down with control to complete one rep
Stand upright with your feet hip-width apart and your hands at your sides or on your hips
Tighten your core and keep your chest lifted
Step forward with one leg, taking a comfortable stride
Lower your body by bending both knees until your front thigh is parallel to the floor
Keep your front knee directly above your ankle and avoid letting it move too far forward
Lower your back knee toward the floor without touching it
Keep your torso upright and your balance steady throughout the movement
Push through the heel of your front foot to return to the starting position
Stand upright with your feet hip-width apart and your hands at your sides or on your hips
Tighten your core and keep your chest lifted

Hold the position with steady breathing and active finger pressure into the floor

Place your free arm out to the side or behind your back for balance
Stack your shoulders over your wrist and keep your arm fully straight
Squeeze your core and glutes to maintain a straight, hollow body line
Shift your weight slightly over your supporting hand and use finger pressure to balance

When you finish the hold, come down with control into a tuck or straddle landing
Start in push-up position with your hands directly under your shoulders and your arms fully straight
Point your fingers outward with your thumbs facing forward
Tighten your core and glutes to prevent your hips from sagging
Without bending your elbows, slowly allow your chest to sink slightly toward the floor by retracting your shoulder blades
Keep the movement small and controlled—only your shoulder blades should move

Fully protract your shoulder blades at the top of the movement while keeping your arms locked straight
Pause briefly, then slowly return to the starting position
Grip the bar with your hands shoulder-width apart and jump or step up to a straight-arm support hold
Lean your torso slightly forward and keep your shoulders depressed away from your ears

Keep your elbows tracking back rather than flaring straight out to the sides
Lower until your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor or your shoulders reach a comfortable depth

Finish each rep with your body controlled and your shoulders stable, not swinging

Place your hands on a wall or railing for balance if needed

Lift your heels as high as you can while keeping your legs straight

Lower your heels back down with control until your feet are flat on the floor
Complete the rep without bouncing at the bottom
Lie face down on the floor with your arms extended overhead and your legs straight
Keep your neck neutral by looking down at the floor
Engage your glutes and lower back and lift your arms, chest, and legs off the floor
Hold briefly at the top while keeping your limbs straight and active
Lower your arms and legs back down with control to complete one rep
Avoid jerking your head back or arching excessively at the lower back
Kneel facing a bench or raised surface with your knees hip-width apart
Place your elbows on the surface shoulder-width apart with your forearms resting on the bench
Clasp your hands together and keep your elbows slightly turned out to create tension through your lats
Walk your knees back until your torso is parallel to the floor with your legs extended behind you
Keep your spine neutral and avoid hiking your hips up or sagging your lower back
Brace your core and draw your lats down and back while breathing steadily
Hold the stretch with active tension through your shoulders and core
When you finish the hold, bend your elbows and sit back onto your heels with control

Place your hands on your chest or extend them forward for balance
Keep your body in a straight line from your knees through your hips to your shoulders
Slowly lean back by extending at the knees while keeping your hips extended

Use your quads to pull yourself back up to the starting upright kneeling position

Complete all reps on one leg before switching sides

Avoid arching your lower back or letting your ribs flare as your limbs extend

Keep your hips forward and your body in a straight line from knees to shoulders

Keep the movement controlled and avoid collapsing at the hips or lower back

Lower your toes back down with control to complete one rep
Keep your hips and back stable against the wall throughout the movement

name: Shoulder Taps
steps: Start in a high push-up position with your hands shoulder-width apart directly under your shoulders
Extend your legs behind you and balance on your toes with your body in a straight line from head to heels
Tighten your core, glutes, and legs to prevent your hips from sagging, piking up, or rotating
Shift your weight slightly onto one hand while keeping both feet grounded
Lift your opposite hand off the floor and tap the shoulder of your supporting arm
Place your hand back under your shoulder and repeat the tap on the other side
Alternate sides each rep while keeping your hips as level and still as possible
Lie on your back with your arms beside you and your legs extended
Press your palms into the floor and lift your hips, bringing your legs overhead toward the floor behind you
Bend your elbows and place your hands on your lower back for support with your upper arms on the floor
Walk your hands up your back toward your shoulder blades to lift your torso higher onto your shoulders
Straighten your legs toward the ceiling, keeping them together with your toes pointed
Press your elbows and upper arms firmly into the floor and keep your neck relaxed with your gaze toward your chest
Engage your core and hold your weight on your shoulders and upper back, not on your neck
To exit, bend your knees toward your forehead and roll down through your spine with control
